title: State-of-the-arts methods for studying factors driving the utilization of open science resources
author: Trinh, Hang, et al.
description: Review Highlights•This study first argues that the utilization of open science resources can be conceptualized in two ways: the process of accepting a new information system and technology, and the process of filtering and adapting new values into an existing value system.•It then reviews commonly used methods, namely the Mindsponge model of value-filtering, the Technology Acceptance Model, the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology, Self-Determination Theory, and Self-Efficacy Theory.•We propose a questionnaire that integrates factors from these methods and presents sample questions for two categories of subjects: university students and university lecturers/staff. Some discussion on applications of statistical methods is also provided.
